title: FSSAI Return Filing Online | Process, Fees & Consultant India

hero.headline: Accurate & Timely FSSAI Return Filing
hero.subtext: Stay compliant with food safety laws. Let Professionals Law handle your D-1 & D-2 return filings.

mainHeading: FSSAI Return Filing Online Service in India

introduction.heading: Introduction and Its Compliance
body: The Food safety and Standards Authority of India (FSSAI) has put in place an obligation on food business operators (FBOs) to submit FSSAI returns on a regular basis, pursuant to the compliance under the Food Safety and Standards (Licensing and Registration) Regulations, 2011. For instance, with FSSAI Returns, food businesses are made to work transparently, are asked to report their business and keep detailed records of food produced, sold and imported. Professionals Law supports companies in India file FSSAI return smoothly and in full compliance of the food safety laws and with the interest of business and consumer in view.

whyNeeded.heading: Why It Is Needed
body: FSSAI return filing is a compulsion under the Food Safety and Standards Act, 2006 for controlling and directing the food business operations. It vouches for the proper record of food production, sales and distribution in order to assist in offering transparency and accountability within businesses. Timely filing not only safeguards against penalties, license suspension or cancellation due to non-compliance but also assures regulators and consumers that the business adheres to the prescribed safety and hygiene standards, thereby reinforcing trust and credibility in the marketplace. By using Professionals Law, you will remain compliant and protect your food business from potential legal challenges.

body.title: Smooth License Renewal
body.description: Regular filing of FSSAI returns simplifies the license renewal process and helps maintain an uninterrupted business operation.

body.title: Transparency & Traceability
body.description: Filing returns ensures accurate record-keeping of food products, production capacity, sales and distribution which promotes transparency.

body.title: Avoid Legal Consequences
body.description: Timely FSSAI return filing avoids heavy penalties, show-cause notices and even license suspension or cancellation.

body.title: Enhances Consumer Confidence
body.description: A compliant food business builds credibility and trust among consumers and business partners.

criteria: All food business operators (FBOs) with FSSAI License (central or state) must file FSSAI returns.
FSSAI Annual Return (Form D-1) is mandatory for manufacturers, importers and exporters.
FSSAI Half-Yearly Return (Form D-2) is applicable to manufacturers of milk and milk products.

categories.applicantType: To file FSSAI returns, businesses typically need the following:
documents: Quantity of food products manufactured or handled
List of products exported or imported
Details of food categories and batch numbers
Invoice copies for purchases and sales
Details of stock held at the beginning and end of the period

body.step: Consultation with Professionals Law’s compliance experts to assess filing requirements
Collection and verification of business activity records, production data and invoices
Preparation of FSSAI Return (Form D-1 and D-2 where applicable)
Filing of the return with FSSAI online portal
Confirmation of successful filing and providing acknowledgment to the client
Ongoing compliance support and reminders for future filings

body.description: Non-filing of FSSAI Returns may attract a penalty of ₹100 per day of default.
Continuous non-compliance can lead to cancellation or suspension of FSSAI License.
Legal prosecution under the Food Safety and Standards Act may also apply in severe cases.

body.question: Who needs to file FSSAI returns?
body.answer: All FSSAI licensed food business operators (manufacturers, importers, exporters, processors) must file annual or half-yearly returns.

body.question: Are FSSAI returns required for FSSAI registration holders?
body.answer: No, FSSAI returns are mandatory only for license holders, not for businesses with basic registration.

body.question: What is Form D-1 and who should file it?
body.answer: Form D-1 is the annual return to be filed by all licensed food manufacturers, importers and exporters.

body.question: What is Form D-2 and who should file it?
body.answer: Form D-2 is the half-yearly return filed by licensed milk and milk product manufacturers.

body.question: What are the penalties for late filing of FSSAI returns?
body.answer: A penalty of ₹100 per day is levied for delay in filing FSSAI returns.

body.question: Can FSSAI returns be revised after filing?
body.answer: There is currently no provision for revision after submission; hence accuracy at the time of filing is crucial.

body.question: What details are required for FSSAI returns?
body.answer: Production, purchase, sales data, quantity handled, product details, batch numbers and export/import information.

body.question: How does Professionals Law help in FSSAI return filing?
body.answer: Professionals Law provides end-to-end assistance, from documentation to accurate preparation and timely filing of FSSAI returns.

body.question: What happens if my license gets suspended for non-filing?
body.answer: You may face business disruption and legal consequences; Professionals Law ensures timely filings to avoid such situations.
